message:
fix: correct hook payload field names and add Apache 2 license

- post-tool-use.sh: extract tool result content per-tool (Read uses
  .file.content, Glob/Grep use .filenames[], Edit shows filepath,
  Task extracts .content[].text). Previous code assumed .content
  existed on all tools, yielding empty results in the UI.

- user-prompt.sh: field is .prompt not .user_prompt. User message
  text was always stored as "".

- stop.sh: no reason/stop_reason field in Stop payload. Use
  last_assistant_message as text fallback. Remove dead session-end
  logic. Drop finish_reason from stored data.

- subagent-stop.sh: defensive .stop_reason // .reason fallback.

- LICENSE: Apache 2.0
- README: fix frontend port (9080 not 3000), add tool response table,
  expand what-gets-recorded section

@@ -1,73 +1,108 @@
-# cxdb-observability Plugin
+# cxdb Claude Code Observability Plugin
 
-Records every Claude Code session as structured `cxdb.ConversationItem` turns in [cxdb](https://github.com/strongdm/cxdb), enabling full trajectory browsing in the cxdb UI.
+Records every [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) session as structured turns in [cxdb](https://github.com/strongdm/cxdb) — a turn-DAG context store for AI agent trajectories. Browse full session histories, tool calls, token usage, and reasoning in the cxdb UI.
 
 ## Requirements
 
-- bash, curl, jq, python3 (all standard on macOS)
-- cxdb running locally or on Tailscale (see [cxdb repo](../cxdb))
+- Claude Code (any recent version)
+- bash, curl, jq, python3 (standard on macOS and most Linux)
+- A running cxdb instance (local Docker or shared Tailscale instance)
 
 ## Quick Start
 
-### 1. Start cxdb locally
+### 1. Start cxdb
 
-In the cxdb repo:
+In the [cxdb repo](https://github.com/strongdm/cxdb):
 
 ```bash
 make dev-docker-up
 ```
 
-Starts cxdb in Docker. Frontend: http://localhost:3000 | API: http://localhost:9010
+Starts cxdb in Docker. Frontend at http://localhost:9080 · API at http://localhost:9010
 
 ### 2. Install the plugin
 
 ```bash
+git clone https://github.com/prime-radiant-inc/claude-plugin-cxdb-integration
+cd claude-plugin-cxdb-integration
 make install
 ```
 
 ### 3. Start a new Claude Code session
 
-Sessions started **after** installation will be recorded automatically. Existing sessions are not affected.
-
-### 4. View trajectories
-
-Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser. Sessions appear as contexts with full turn history.
+Sessions started **after** installation are recorded automatically. Open http://localhost:9080 to watch them appear in real time.
 
 ## How It Works
 
-Seven Claude Code hooks (bash + curl + jq) fire at session lifecycle events:
+Seven Claude Code hooks record the full session lifecycle:
 
-| Hook | cxdb Type | Notes |
+| Hook | cxdb item\_type | Notes |
 |---|---|---|
-| `SessionStart` | `system` (info) | Creates context, registers type bundle, writes ContextMetadata with Provenance |
-| `UserPromptSubmit` | `user_input` | Async |
-| `PostToolUse` | *(buffered)* | Async — no cxdb write yet |
-| `Stop` | `assistant_turn` | Flushes buffered tool calls + parses transcript for text/metrics |
-| `SubagentStop` | `handoff` | Records agent-to-agent handoffs |
-| `PreCompact` | `system` (warning) | Async |
-| `Notification` | `system` (info) | Async |
-
-All cxdb writes are fire-and-forget — if cxdb is down, your session continues normally.
+| `SessionStart` | `system` (info) | Creates context, publishes type bundle, writes ContextMetadata + Provenance |
+| `UserPromptSubmit` | `user_input` | Captures user message text (async) |
+| `PostToolUse` | *(buffered)* | Buffers tool call + result to session state — no cxdb write yet |
+| `Stop` | `assistant_turn` | Flushes buffered tool calls; parses transcript for text + token metrics |
+| `SubagentStop` | `handoff` | Records agent-to-agent handoffs for Task tool calls |
+| `PreCompact` | `system` (warning) | Notes when context window compaction occurs (async) |
+| `Notification` | `system` (info) | Records Claude Code notifications (async) |
+
+Tool call results are captured per tool type — each tool's response has a different structure:
+
+| Tool | Content captured |
+|---|---|
+| `Bash` | stdout (+ stderr if non-empty) |
+| `Read` | File contents (capped at 4000 chars) |
+| `Glob` | Matched file paths |
+| `Grep` | Matched file paths or content lines |
+| `Edit` | `"Edited <filepath>"` |
+| `Task` | Subagent's final text output (capped at 4000 chars) |
+| `WebFetch` / `WebSearch` | Generic fallback |
+
+All cxdb writes are fire-and-forget — if cxdb is unreachable, your Claude Code session continues normally.
 
 ## Configuration
 
 ### Pointing at a different cxdb instance
 
-To use a shared team cxdb instance instead of localhost, create `~/.claude/cxdb-observability.local.md`:
+Create `~/.claude/cxdb-observability.local.md` with YAML frontmatter:
 
 ```
 url: http://cxdb.your-tailnet.ts.net:9010
 ```
 
-Or set the `CXDB_URL` environment variable.
+Or set the `CXDB_URL` environment variable before starting Claude Code.
 
-**URL priority:**
+**URL resolution order:**
 1. `$CXDB_URL` environment variable
 2. `url:` field in `~/.claude/cxdb-observability.local.md`
 3. `http://localhost:9010` (default)
 
-## Uninstall
+## What Gets Recorded
+
+Each session produces a chain of `cxdb.ConversationItem` turns:
+
+```
+system (info)        ← Session Started, with ContextMetadata + Provenance
+  user_input         ← "Write a function that..."
+    assistant_turn   ← Text + tool calls (Bash, Read, Edit, ...) + token metrics
+      user_input     ← "looks good, now add tests"
+        assistant_turn
+          ...
+```
+
+**Provenance** fields captured per session:
+- Git user name + email
+- Hostname, architecture, OS user
+- Session ID, working directory, permission mode
+- Parent/root context IDs for subagent lineage
+
+## Install / Uninstall
 
 ```bash
-make uninstall
+make install      # registers plugin in ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json
+make uninstall    # removes it
 ```
+
+## License
+
+Apache 2.0 — see [LICENSE](LICENSE).

@@ -4,11 +4,16 @@
 # stop.sh flushes all buffered tool calls as one AssistantTurn per turn.
 #
 # Actual PostToolUse payload structure (discovered via live probe):
-#   .tool_response.stdout   — Bash stdout
-#   .tool_response.stderr   — Bash stderr
-#   .tool_response.content  — non-Bash tools (Read, Write, etc.)
-#   .tool_response.is_error — present on tool errors
-#   .tool_use_id            — always present at root level
+#   .tool_use_id                       — always present at root level
+#   .tool_response.is_error            — present on tool errors
+#   Bash:   .tool_response.{stdout,stderr,interrupted}
+#   Read:   .tool_response.file.{filePath,content,numLines,totalLines}
+#   Glob:   .tool_response.{filenames[],numFiles,durationMs,truncated}
+#   Grep:   .tool_response.{mode,filenames[],numFiles}  (files_with_matches)
+#           .tool_response.{mode,content}               (content mode)
+#   Edit:   .tool_response.{filePath,structuredPatch[],userModified}
+#   Write:  unknown — generic fallback used
+#   Task:   .tool_response.{status,content[{type,text}],totalDurationMs,totalTokens}
 set -euo pipefail
 
 source "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/lib.sh"
@@ -77,18 +82,58 @@ case "$tool_name" in
     ;;
 esac
 
-# Extract content from tool_response:
-# - Bash: stdout field (append stderr if non-empty)
-# - Other tools: content field
-# - Error case: stderr or content
-content=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '
-  .tool_response |
-  if .stdout != null then
-    (.stdout // "") + (if (.stderr // "") != "" then "\n[stderr]: " + .stderr else "" end)
-  else
-    (.content // "")
-  end
-')
+# Extract content from tool_response — format varies by tool (see header comments)
+content_truncated=false
+case "$tool_name" in
+  Bash)
+    content=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '
+      .tool_response |
+      (.stdout // "") + (if (.stderr // "") != "" then "\n[stderr]: " + .stderr else "" end)
+    ')
+    ;;
+  Read)
+    content=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_response.file.content // ""')
+    if [ "${#content}" -gt 4000 ]; then
+      content="${content:0:4000}"
+      content_truncated=true
+    fi
+    ;;
+  Glob)
+    content=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '(.tool_response.filenames // []) | join("\n")')
+    ;;
+  Grep)
+    content=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '
+      if .tool_response.filenames != null then
+        (.tool_response.filenames | join("\n"))
+      elif (.tool_response.content | type) == "string" then
+        .tool_response.content
+      else "" end
+    ')
+    ;;
+  Edit)
+    content=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '
+      "Edited " + (.tool_response.filePath // (.tool_input.file_path // "file"))
+    ')
+    ;;
+  Task)
+    content=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '
+      [.tool_response.content[]? | select(.type == "text") | .text] | join("")
+    ')
+    if [ "${#content}" -gt 4000 ]; then
+      content="${content:0:4000}"
+      content_truncated=true
+    fi
+    ;;
+  *)
+    # Generic fallback: try common output fields
+    content=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '
+      .tool_response |
+      if .stdout != null then .stdout
+      elif (.content | type) == "string" then .content
+      else "" end
+    ')
+    ;;
+esac
 
 # Build ToolCallItem based on success/error
 if [ "$is_error" = "true" ]; then
@@ -118,6 +163,7 @@ else
     --arg args "$args_str" \
     --arg desc "$desc" \
     --arg content "$content" \
+    --argjson content_truncated "$content_truncated" \
     --argjson duration_ms "$duration_ms" \
     '{
       "id": $id,
@@ -128,6 +174,7 @@ else
       "duration_ms": $duration_ms,
       "result": {
         "content": $content,
+        "content_truncated": $content_truncated,
         "success": true,
         "exit_code": null
       }

@@ -1,7 +1,13 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 # stop.sh — Stop hook (synchronous)
-# Fires at the end of every turn. Parses transcript for the current turn's
-# assistant text and metrics, then flushes buffered tool calls as one AssistantTurn.
+# Fires at the end of every turn. Parses transcript for metrics, then flushes
+# buffered tool calls as one AssistantTurn.
+#
+# ACTUAL PAYLOAD FIELDS (confirmed via live probe):
+#   session_id, cwd, permission_mode, hook_event_name, stop_hook_active,
+#   last_assistant_message   ← full text of last assistant response
+#
+# NOTE: No "reason"/"stop_reason" field exists in the Stop payload.
 #
 # TRANSCRIPT FORMAT (discovered via _probe-stop.sh):
 #   type=assistant entries; grouped by message.id (one group = one API call).
@@ -16,7 +22,10 @@ session_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id')
 context_id=$(get_context_id "$session_id")
 [ -z "$context_id" ] && exit 0
 
-reason=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.reason // ""')
+# last_assistant_message is the full text of the last assistant response.
+# Used as fallback if transcript parsing yields no text.
+last_assistant_message=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.last_assistant_message // ""')
+
 ts=$(now_ms)
 turn_number=$(get_turn_number "$session_id")
 tool_calls=$(get_tool_calls "$session_id")
@@ -138,6 +147,11 @@ PYEOF
   new_line_count=$(echo "$transcript_data" | jq -r '.line_count // 0')
 fi
 
+# Fall back to last_assistant_message if transcript parsing yielded no text
+if [ -z "$assistant_text" ] && [ -n "$last_assistant_message" ]; then
+  assistant_text="$last_assistant_message"
+fi
+
 # ---- Update transcript_line_count in state ----
 state_now=$(read_state "$session_id")
 if [ -n "$state_now" ] && [ "$new_line_count" -gt "$start_line" ]; then
@@ -165,7 +179,6 @@ turn_payload=$(jq -n \
   --argjson tool_calls "$tool_calls" \
   --argjson metrics "$metrics" \
   --argjson turn_number "$turn_number" \
-  --arg finish_reason "$reason" \
   --argjson ts "$ts" \
   '{
     "type_id": "cxdb.ConversationItem",
@@ -180,40 +193,13 @@ turn_payload=$(jq -n \
         "reasoning": $reasoning,
         "metrics": $metrics,
         "agent": "claude-code",
-        "turn_number": $turn_number,
-        "finish_reason": $finish_reason
+        "turn_number": $turn_number
       }
     }
   }')
 
 cxdb_append "$context_id" "$turn_payload"
 
-# ---- Append session-end SystemMessage on final stop ----
-# "end_turn" or "max_turns" with no pending tool calls = final stop.
-if [ "$reason" = "end_turn" ] || [ "$reason" = "max_turns" ]; then
-  tool_count=$(echo "$tool_calls" | jq 'length')
-  if [ "$tool_count" = "0" ]; then
-    end_payload=$(jq -n \
-      --arg reason "$reason" \
-      --argjson ts "$ts" \
-      '{
-        "type_id": "cxdb.ConversationItem",
-        "type_version": 3,
-        "data": {
-          "item_type": "system",
-          "status": "complete",
-          "timestamp": $ts,
-          "system": {
-            "kind": "info",
-            "title": "Session Ended",
-            "content": ("Stop reason: " + $reason)
-          }
-        }
-      }')
-    cxdb_append "$context_id" "$end_payload"
-  fi
-fi
-
 # ---- Flush: increment turn_number, clear tool_calls ----
 flush_turn "$session_id" || true

@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ context_id=$(get_context_id "$session_id")
 
 # Try common field names for the subagent session ID (verify with probe)
 subagent_session_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.subagent_session_id // .child_session_id // ""')
-reason=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.reason // "completed"')
+reason=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.stop_reason // .reason // "completed"')
 ts=$(now_ms)
 
 # Build the to_agent identifier: use subagent context_id if we have it.

@@ -11,7 +11,8 @@ session_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id')
 context_id=$(get_context_id "$session_id")
 [ -z "$context_id" ] && exit 0
 
-user_prompt=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.user_prompt // ""')
+# Actual payload field is "prompt" (not "user_prompt")
+user_prompt=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')
 # synthetic is a bool in the payload (or absent); default false
 synthetic=$(echo "$input" | jq '.synthetic // false')
 ts=$(now_ms)
